evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Identify opportunities to improve transformation pipeline efficiency and reliability
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: One reliability signal is balanced against fintech-scaleup speed framing ('support data infrastructure scaling', junior fast-growth environment), giving parity rather than a dominant orientation.
evidence.domain_risk: Contribute to data models supporting credit decisions, origination, portfolio analysis, and investor reporting
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role directly supports credit decisions and investor reporting at a fintech lender, matching high domain risk per the default-high fintech sector plus explicit financial-reporting language.
evidence.collaboration_width: analysts; product teams; backend engineers; data platform team
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build and improve dbt models and transformations with senior engineer guidance
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Snowflake, Fivetran) appear in responsibilities with an existing data platform team and senior engineers providing guidance, indicating a mid-stage, growing data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Contribute to data models supporting credit decisions, origination, portfolio analysis, and investor reporting; Build and improve dbt models and transformations with senior engineer guidance
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities name specific tools, domains, and precise application context (dbt models, credit decisions, investor reporting), letting the reader understand the actual day-to-day work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Contribute to data models supporting credit decisions, origination, portfolio analysis, and investor reporting
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The named outputs (credit decisions, portfolio analysis, investor reporting) are finance-domain deliverables, matching finance orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Build and improve dbt models and transformations with senior engineer guidance
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: As a graduate/junior role explicitly built 'with senior engineer guidance,' the candidate receives scoped work and delivers it rather than setting direction, matching execution.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to be comfortable using AI tools (the tech stack even names Claude) to accelerate their own engineering work, matching the ai_user pattern.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal present.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No mention of dbt tests, data contracts, Great Expectations, Soda, or explicit data quality frameworks.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Identify opportunities to improve transformation pipeline efficiency and reliability
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Pipeline reliability is named as a concern but secondary to delivery and growth framing, and there is no compliance/regulatory register despite the fintech/investor-reporting context, keeping this moderate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Support data infrastructure scaling; Build and improve dbt models and transformations with senior engineer guidance
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both scales existing infrastructure and improves/builds dbt transformations, giving a mixed greenfield/fix signal.
evidence.loss_aversion: "Identify opportunities to improve transformation pipeline efficiency and reliability" — reliability is a stated concern but secondary to the growth and delivery framing dominating the JD.
